terms.glossary_definition: Two [type]s that unify are said to be [unifiable type]s.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_kv533rntni1x Any two [binding]s with the same name in the two [pattern-without-alternation]s shall have [unifiable type]s and shall have the same [binding mode]s.

terms.glossary_definition: An adjusted call operand is a call operand adjusted with inserted [borrow expression]s and [dereference expression]s.
terms.chapter_definition: An adjusted call operand is the call operand after any implicit adjustments required by call resolution.

terms.glossary_definition: The alignment of a value specifies which addresses are valid for storing the value.
terms.chapter_definition: All [value]s have an alignment and a size.

terms.glossary_definition: An arithmetic overflow occurs if an arithmetic expression or a negation expression computes a value of a scalar type that lies outside of the range of valid [value]s for the scalar type.
terms.chapter_definition: An arithmetic overflow occurs when an operator expression computes a value of a scalar type that lies outside of the range of valid [value]s for the scalar type or when one or more operand of an operator expression lies outside of the range of valid [value]s for the operation.

terms.glossary_definition: An arity is the number of [tuple field]s in a tuple type.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_w369n8lmwr7g If the subpattern succeeds the RecordStructRestPattern, then its position is the position within the PatternList list in right-to-left order, starting from the arity of the deconstructee minus one.

terms.glossary_definition: An associated item is an item that appears within an implementation or a trait.
terms.chapter_definition: #. fls_y20pmwo3v3uu If the macro invocation appears as part of an associated item, an item within an external block, or another macro invocation, the output is required to constitute zero or more [item]s.

terms.glossary_definition: An associated type is a type alias that appears as an associated item.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_y9bc691kkh6v Otherwise the type is associated type core::ops::Deref::Target.

terms.glossary_definition: Associativity is the order by which [operand]s are evaluated within a single expression.
terms.chapter_definition: Certain [expression]s are subject to precedence and associativity.

terms.glossary_definition: An async closure type is a unique anonymous function type that encapsulates all [capture target]s of a closure expression producing a core::future::Future.
terms.chapter_definition: An async closure type is the closure type defined by an async closure expression.

terms.glossary_definition: An async control flow boundary is a control flow boundary that additionally allows the suspension of execution via [await expression]s.
terms.chapter_definition: An async control flow boundary is a control flow boundary introduced by an async block expression, async function, or async closure expression.

terms.glossary_definition: An async function is a function subject to keyword ``async``.
terms.chapter_definition: An async control flow boundary is a control flow boundary introduced by an async block expression, async function, or async closure expression.

terms.glossary_definition: An attribute is a general, free-form metadatum that is interpreted based on its name, convention, language, and tool.
terms.chapter_definition: Attribute cfg enables conditional compilation.

terms.glossary_definition: An attribute content is a construct that provides the content of an attribute.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_wtol98rrqka5 Fragment specifier **meta** requires an attribute content.

terms.glossary_definition: A binary crate is a crate whose crate type is ``bin``.
terms.chapter_definition: Attribute windows_subsystem shall apply to the crate root module of a binary crate.

terms.glossary_definition: A binary operator is an operator that operates on two [operand]s.
terms.chapter_definition: A left operand is an operand that appears on the left-hand side of a binary operator.

terms.glossary_definition: A binding argument is a generic argument that supplies the type of an associated trait type.
terms.chapter_definition: A binding argument is conformant with an associated type when the supplied type of the binding argument fulfills the required [trait bound]s of the associated type.

terms.glossary_definition: Binding mode is the mechanism by which a matched value is bound to a binding of a pattern.
terms.chapter_definition: A binding mode describes whether a binding captures a matched value by value, by reference, or by mutable reference.

terms.glossary_definition: A borrow is a reference produced by borrowing.
terms.chapter_definition: A raw borrow expression is an expression that creates a raw pointer to the memory location of its operand without incurring a borrow; this raw pointer is a pointer of a raw pointer type.

terms.glossary_definition: A memory location is borrowed when a reference pointing to it is active.
terms.chapter_definition: When the operand of a borrow expression is a place expression, the borrow expression produces a reference to the memory location indicated by the operand. The memory location is placed in a borrowed state, or simply borrowed.

terms.glossary_definition: A bound imposes a constraint on a generic parameter by limiting the set of possible [generic substitution]s.

terms.glossary_definition: C is the programming language described in the ISO/IEC 9899:2018 International Standard.
terms.chapter_definition: A variadic part indicates the presence of C-like optional parameters.

terms.glossary_definition: Call conformance measures the compatibility between a set of [argument operand]s and a set if [function parameter]s or [field]s.
terms.chapter_definition: A call conformance is the requirement that a call expression uses the ABI of the invoked function.

terms.glossary_definition: The capturing environment of a capturing expression consists of all [capture target]s that are defined outside the capturing expression.
terms.chapter_definition: The capturing environment of a capturing expression consists of the [value]s of all captured [capture target]s.

terms.glossary_definition: char is a type whose [value]s denote Unicode characters.
terms.chapter_definition: A char is a type whose [value]s are represented as a 32-bit unsigned word in the 0x000 - 0xD7FF or the 0xE000 - 0x10FFFF inclusive ranges of Unicode.

terms.glossary_definition: In Unicode, a code point is a numeric value that maps to a character.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_eb00s8fxlvjb Convert an operand of type char to the value of the corresponding code point, followed by a numeric cast.

terms.glossary_definition: A comment is a lexical element that acts as an annotation or an explanation in program text.
terms.chapter_definition: Each of the special characters listed for single character punctuator is a simple punctuator except if this character is used as a character of a compound punctuator, or a character of a character literal, a comment, a numeric literal, or a string literal.

terms.glossary_definition: A concrete type is a type described by a type specification.
terms.chapter_definition: Attribute type_length_limit sets the maximum number of [generic substitution]s for [type parameter]s when constructing a concrete type.

terms.glossary_definition: Conditional compilation is the process of compiling conditionally-compiled source code.
terms.chapter_definition: Attribute cfg enables conditional compilation.

terms.glossary_definition: A constant is an immutable value whose uses are substituted by the value.

terms.glossary_definition: A constant argument is a generic argument that supplies the value of a constant parameter.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_3of516eo0kkx The constant argument for a constant parameter,

terms.glossary_definition: A constant function is a function subject to keyword ``const``.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_490a1b74fut6 [Call expression]s where the callee is a constant function,

terms.glossary_definition: A constant initializer is a construct that provides the value of its related constant.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_ljc6jq5ksbcs The constant initializer of an associated constant or a constant,

terms.glossary_definition: A constant parameter is a generic parameter for a constant.
terms.chapter_definition: A constant argument is conformant with a constant parameter when the [type]s of the constant argument and the constant parameter are unifiable.

terms.glossary_definition: A constant parameter initializer is a construct that provides the default `value of its related constant parameter.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_OhVxhJ23x7W2 Otherwise, if the constant parameter has a constant parameter initializer, then the value is that of the constant parameter initializer.

terms.glossary_definition: A generic parameter is said to constrain an implementation if it makes the [implementation]'s applicability more narrow.

terms.glossary_definition: A construct is a piece of program text that is an instance of a syntactic category.
terms.chapter_definition: A where clause is a construct that specifies [bound]s on [lifetime parameter]s and [type]s that have to hold for the construct subject to the where clause to be valid.

terms.glossary_definition: A container operand is an operand that indicates the value whose field is selected in a field access expression.
terms.chapter_definition: A candidate container type is the type of the container operand of a field access expression under resolution.

terms.glossary_definition: A control flow boundary is a construct that limits control flow from returning beyond the construct, and acts as the target of control flow returning operations.
terms.chapter_definition: A control flow boundary is a construct that provides the target of a return expression and bounds its control flow transfer.

terms.glossary_definition: A copy type is a type that implements the core::marker::Copy trait.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_hLTnHnZuaHve A copy type, or

terms.glossary_definition: A crate is a unit of compilation and linking that contains a tree of nested [module]s.
terms.chapter_definition: [Procedural macro]s shall be defined in a crate subject to attribute crate_type where the type is ``proc-macro``.

terms.glossary_definition: A crate root is an entry point into a crate.
terms.chapter_definition: A crate root is the crate root module of a crate.

terms.notes: Term defined inline within another paragraph; definition is contextual
terms.glossary_definition: A crate root module is the root of the nested module tree of a crate.
terms.chapter_definition: An external prelude is a prelude that brings in scope of the crate root module the entities of the [crate]s imported using external [crate import]s or supplied by a tool.

terms.glossary_definition: A value of an indirection type is dangling if it is either null or not all of the bytes at the referred memory location are part of the same allocation.
terms.chapter_definition: It is undefined behavior to dereference a raw pointer that is either dangling or unaligned.

terms.glossary_definition: Default representation is a type representation that does not make any guarantees about layout.
terms.chapter_definition: Default representation makes no guarantees about the layout.

terms.glossary_definition: A derive macro is a procedural macro that consumes a stream of [token]s and produces a stream of tokens, and is invoked via attribute derive.
terms.chapter_definition: A derive macro is a procedural macro that consumes a stream of [token]s and produces a stream of [token]s. [Derive macro]s are used to construct new syntax for [abstract data type]s.

terms.glossary_definition: A destructor is a function that is invoked immediately before the destruction of a value of a drop type.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_tms5r9f5ogcb The type of the value expression does not have a destructor, and

terms.glossary_definition: A direction modifier is a construct that indicates whether a register argument initializes a register, assigns the value of a register to an expression, or both.
terms.chapter_definition: An input register is a register whose register name is used in a register argument subject to direction modifier ``in``, ``inout``, or ``inlateout``.

terms.glossary_definition: A discriminant is an opaque integer that identifies an enum variant.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_le6bchl25ewz An operand of an enum type and a target integer type perform enum cast. An enum cast converts the operand to its discriminant, followed by a numeric cast.

terms.glossary_definition: A discriminant initializer provides the value of a discriminant.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_66m2hwkju0vv The discriminant initializer of an enum variant,

terms.glossary_definition: Elaboration is the process by which a declaration achieves its runtime effects.
terms.chapter_definition: The elaboration of a constant evaluates its constant initializer.

terms.glossary_definition: An element type is the type of the elements of an array type or a slice type.
terms.chapter_definition: The type of an array expression is ``[T; N]``, where ``T`` is the element type and ``N`` is the size of the array. The size of an array is determined as follows:

terms.glossary_definition: An entity is a construct that can be referred to within program text, usually via a field access expression or a path.
terms.chapter_definition: A path segment shall resolve to exactly one entity.

terms.glossary_definition: An enum value is a value of an enum type.
terms.chapter_definition: A struct expression is an expression that constructs an enum value, a struct value, or a union value.

terms.glossary_definition: An enum variant value is the enum value of the corresponding enum of the enum variant.
terms.chapter_definition: A record struct pattern is a pattern that matches a enum variant value, a struct value, or a union value.

terms.glossary_definition: An escaped character is the textual representation for a character with special meaning. An escaped character consists of character 0x5C (reverse solidus), followed by the single character encoding of the special meaning character. For example, ``\t`` is the escaped character for 0x09 (horizontal ta

terms.glossary_definition: Evaluation is the process by which an expression achieves its runtime effects.
terms.chapter_definition: The evaluation of an equals expression proceeds as follows:

terms.glossary_definition: Execution is the process by which a statement achieves its runtime effects.
terms.chapter_definition: The execution of an empty statement has no effect.

terms.glossary_definition: An exported function is an export of a function.
terms.chapter_definition: It is undefined behavior when attribute track_caller is applied to an exported function but the external function it links to is missing the attribute.

terms.glossary_definition: An exported static is an export of a static.
terms.chapter_definition: An exported static is a static subject to attribute no_mangle.

terms.glossary_definition: An expression is a construct that produces a value, and may have side effects at run-time.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_htLp5J5ObgNh The expression of a constant initializer.

terms.glossary_definition: An expression-with-block is an expression whose structure involves a block expression.
terms.chapter_definition: An item declared within the block expression of an expression-with-block is in scope within the related block expression.

terms.glossary_definition: An expression-without-block is an expression whose structure does not involve a block expression.
terms.chapter_definition: #. fls_nsh2vwx8oiw If the macro invocation appears as part of an expression-without-block, the output is required to constitute an expression.

terms.glossary_definition: An external function is an unchecked import of a foreign function.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_gu3331rmv2ho Any call to an external function,

terms.glossary_definition: An external function item type is a function item type where the related function is an external function.
terms.chapter_definition: A call expression whose callee type is either an external function item type, an unsafe function item type, or an unsafe function pointer type shall require unsafe context.

terms.glossary_definition: An external static is an import of a foreign variable.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_AR8ZIYlDRSNs Any interaction with an external static,

terms.glossary_definition: A field is an element of an abstract data type.
terms.chapter_definition: An indexed deconstructor is a construct that matches the position of a field.

terms.glossary_definition: A field index is the position of a field within a tuple struct type or tuple enum variant. The first field has a field index of zero, the Nth field has a field index of N-1.
terms.chapter_definition: An indexed initializer matches a field of the constructee when the field index of the indexed initializer resolves to a valid position of a field in the constructee. Such an indexed initializer is a matched indexed initializer.

terms.glossary_definition: A floating-point type is a numeric type whose [value]s denote fractional numbers.
terms.chapter_definition: #. fls_NcLf4o1dpniS If the type of both [operand]s is the same integer type or floating-point type, then the addition expression evaluates to the sum of the [operand]s, following the rules of unsigned integer addition for [unsigned integer type]s, two's complement addition for [signed integer type]s

terms.glossary_definition: A function is a value of a function type that models a behavior.
terms.chapter_definition: A procedural macro is a macro that encapsulates syntactic transformations in a function. [Procedural macro]s consume one or more streams of [token]s and produce a stream of [token]s.

terms.glossary_definition: A function body is the block expression of a function.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_Sowatt1V988J A function body.

terms.glossary_definition: A function parameter is a construct that yields a set of [binding]s that bind matched input [value]s to [name]s at the site of a call expression or a method call expression.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_etyo9bmzxby6 It shall have a single function parameter whose type specification indicates type proc_macro::TokenStream,

terms.glossary_definition: A function signature is a unique identification of a function that encompasses of its [function qualifier]s, name, [generic parameter]s, [function parameter]s, return type, and where clause.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_ulcdetwp6x96 The source type is a function item type, the target type is a function pointer type and the source's function signature is a subtype of the target's function signature.

terms.glossary_definition: A trait or type is fundamental when its implementation coherence rules are relaxed and the trait or type is always treated as if it was a local trait or a local type.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_3tbm20k2ixol ``Trait`` is fundamental or a local trait (a trait defined in the current crate), or

terms.glossary_definition: A future represents a value of a type that implements the core::future::Future trait which may not have finished computing yet.
terms.chapter_definition: An await expression is an expression that polls a future, suspending the execution of the future until the future is ready.

terms.glossary_definition: A generic function is a function with [generic parameter]s.
terms.chapter_definition: A static defined within a generic function exists once in the output executable or library.

terms.glossary_definition: A generic substitution is the replacement of a generic parameter with a generic argument.
terms.chapter_definition: A type parameter type is a placeholder type of a type parameter to be substituted by generic substitution.

terms.glossary_definition: A generic type is a type with a generic parameter.
terms.chapter_definition: Variance is a property of [lifetime parameter]s and [type parameter]s that describes the circumstances under which a generic type is a subtype of an instantiation of itself with different [generic argument]s.

terms.glossary_definition: A glob import is a use import that brings all [name]s with public visibility prefixed by its path prefix into scope.
terms.chapter_definition: A glob import is a use import that brings all entities exported by the module or enum its import path prefix resolves to into scope.

terms.glossary_definition: An identifier is hygienic when it has definition site hygiene.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_dz2mvodl818d Definition site hygiene, which resolves to a MacroRulesDeclaration site. [Identifier]s with definition site hygiene cannot reference the environment of the MacroRulesDeclaration, cannot be referenced by the environment of a MacroInvocation, and are considered hygienic.

terms.glossary_definition: An identifier is a lexical element that refers to a name.
terms.chapter_definition: A named initializer matches a field of the constructee when its identifier and the name of the field are the same. Such a named initializer is a matched named initializer.

terms.glossary_definition: A value is immutable when it cannot be modified.
terms.chapter_definition: [Binding]s introduced in the pattern of a match arm matcher are immutable in the match arm guard.

terms.glossary_definition: An immutable borrow is an immutable reference produced by borrowing.
terms.chapter_definition: fls_MT69AGRXH9pS` An implicit borrow may be an immutable borrow or a mutable borrow if required.

terms.glossary_definition: An immutable place expression context is a place expression context whose memory location cannot be modified.
terms.chapter_definition: An immutable place expression context is a place expression context that may evaluate its operand as an immutable memory location. All [place expression context]s that are not [mutable place expression context]s are [immutable place expression context]s.

terms.glossary_definition: An immutable reference is a value of a shared reference type, and prevents the mutation of its referent.
terms.chapter_definition: A captured capture target with capture mode by immutable reference capture binds an immutable reference to the capture target and passes the immutable reference into the capturing environment.

terms.glossary_definition: An implementation is an item that supplements an implementing type by extending its functionality.
terms.chapter_definition: A generic implementation is an implementation with [generic parameter]s.

terms.glossary_definition: An implementation body is a construct that encapsulates the [associated item]s, [inner attribute]s, and [inner doc comment]s of an implementation.
terms.chapter_definition: A generic parameter of an implementation is in scope within the related implementation body and where clause.

terms.glossary_definition: Implementation conformance measures the compatibility between a trait implementation and the implemented trait.
terms.chapter_definition: A trait implementation exhibits implementation conformance when it satisfies the constraints of its implemented trait.

terms.glossary_definition: An implemented trait is a trait whose functionality has been implemented by an implementing type.
terms.chapter_definition: A trait implementation exhibits implementation conformance when it satisfies the constraints of its implemented trait.

terms.glossary_definition: An implicit borrow is a borrow that is not present syntactically in program text.

terms.glossary_definition: A name is in scope when it can be referred to.
terms.chapter_definition: A scope is a region of program text where an entity can be referred to. An entity is in scope when it can be referred to.

terms.glossary_definition: An index expression is an expression that indexes into a value of a type.

terms.glossary_definition: An integer type is a numeric type whose [value]s denote whole numbers.

terms.glossary_definition: An irrefutable pattern is a pattern that always matches the value it is being matched against.
terms.chapter_definition: If a let statement lacks a block expression, then the pattern of the let statement shall be an irrefutable pattern.

terms.glossary_definition: An item is the most basic semantic element in program text. An item defines the compile- and run-time semantics of a program.
terms.chapter_definition: The second function parameter of the macro implementation function captures the token stream produced from the related item, including all [outer attribute]s that apply to that item.

terms.glossary_definition: An iteration expression is an expression that provides the criterion of a while loop expression.
terms.chapter_definition: A while loop expression is a loop expression that continues to evaluate its loop body as long as its iteration expression holds true.

terms.glossary_definition: A lifetime parameter is a generic parameter for a lifetime.
terms.chapter_definition: An outlives bound is a trait bound which requires that a lifetime parameter or type outlives a lifetime parameter.

terms.glossary_definition: A main function signature is a function signature subject to specific restrictions.
terms.chapter_definition: A binary crate that is not subject to attribute ``no_main`` shall have a function in scope of its crate root module under the name ``main`` with a main function signature.

terms.glossary_definition: A method is an associated function with a self parameter.
terms.chapter_definition: A method call expression is an expression that invokes a method of a variable.

terms.glossary_definition: Mutability determines whether a construct can modify a value.

terms.glossary_definition: A value is mutable when it can be modified.

terms.glossary_definition: A mutable reference is a value of a mutable reference type, and allows the mutation of its referent.
terms.chapter_definition: A captured capture target with capture mode by mutable reference capture binds a mutable reference to the capture target and passes the mutable reference into the capturing environment.

terms.glossary_definition: A mutable static is a static whose value can be modified.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_ilaqmj3hc5uv A path expression that resolves to a mutable static,

terms.glossary_definition: A mutable variable is a variable whose value can be modified.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_m0gbw9myylv2 A path expression that resolves to a mutable variable that is not currently borrowed,

terms.glossary_definition: A name is an identifier that refers to an entity.
terms.chapter_definition: Visibility is a property of [field]s and [item]s that determines which [module]s can refer to the name of the field or item.

terms.glossary_definition: A named block expression is a block expression with a label.
terms.chapter_definition: A named block expression is a block expression with a label, which names the block for break expression control flow.

terms.glossary_definition: A trait is object safe when it can be used as a trait object type.

terms.glossary_definition: An operand is an expression nested within an expression.

terms.glossary_definition: An opt-out trait bound is a trait bound with Punctuation ``?`` that nullifies an implicitly added trait bound.
terms.chapter_definition: A type parameter has an implicit core::marker::Sized bound, unless an opt-out trait bound for the core::marker::Sized trait is present.

terms.glossary_definition: A path expression is a path that acts as an expression.
terms.chapter_definition: A path expression is an expression that denotes a path.

terms.glossary_definition: A pattern-without-alternation is a pattern that cannot be alternated.
terms.chapter_definition: If the pattern-without-alternation of a let statement is an extending pattern, then the drop scope of the expression of the let statement is extended to the drop scope of the block expression that contains the let statement.

terms.glossary_definition: A pattern-without-range is a pattern-without-alternation that excludes [range pattern]s.
terms.chapter_definition: #. fls_tu6kmwm4v9nj If the macro invocation appears as part of a pattern-without-range, the output is required to constitute a [pattern].

terms.glossary_definition: A pointer is a value of a pointer type.
terms.chapter_definition: An address-to-pointer cast produces a pointer that interprets the integer as a machine address.

terms.glossary_definition: Primitive representation is the type representation of [integer type]s.
terms.chapter_definition: Primitive representation is the type representation of individual [integer type]s. Primitive representation applies only to an enum type that is not a zero-variant enum type. It is possible to combine C representation and primitive representation.

terms.glossary_definition: A qualified path expression is a path expression that resolves through a qualified type.
terms.chapter_definition: A path pattern expressed as a qualified path expression shall refer to an associated constant.

terms.glossary_definition: A receiver operand is an operand that denotes the value whose method is being invoked by a method call expression.
terms.chapter_definition: A receiver type is the type of the receiver operand of a method call expression.

terms.glossary_definition: A record enum variant is an enum variant with a RecordStructFieldList.
terms.chapter_definition: If the constructee is a record enum variant or a record struct, then

terms.glossary_definition: A record struct is a struct with a RecordStructFieldList.
terms.chapter_definition: If the constructee is a record enum variant or a record struct, then

terms.glossary_definition: A recursive type is a type that may define other types within its type specification.
terms.chapter_definition: A recursive type is a type whose contained [type]s refer back to the containing type, either directly or by referring to another type which refers back to the original recursive type.

terms.glossary_definition: A reference is a value of a reference type.
terms.chapter_definition: When the operand of a borrow expression is a place expression, the borrow expression produces a reference to the memory location indicated by the operand. The memory location is placed in a borrowed state, or simply borrowed.

terms.glossary_definition: A reference identifier pattern is an identifier pattern with keyword ``ref``.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_965wt48ooqyw A reference identifier pattern, or

terms.glossary_definition: A refutable type is a type that has more than one value.
terms.chapter_definition: A refutable constant is a constant of a refutable type.

terms.glossary_definition: A renaming provides an alternative name for an existing name.
terms.chapter_definition: A simple import is a use import that brings all entities it refers to into scope, optionally with a different name than they are declared with by using a renaming.

terms.glossary_definition: A representation modifier is a construct that modifies the alignment of a type.
terms.chapter_definition: Type representation may be specified using attribute [repr] and modified further using attribute [repr]'s Alignment representation modifiers. A representation modifier shall apply only to a struct type or a union type subject to C representation or default representation.

terms.glossary_definition: A return type is the type of the result a function returns.
terms.chapter_definition: The return type of a closure type is determined as follows:

terms.glossary_definition: A right operand is an operand that appears on the right-hand side of a binary operator.
terms.chapter_definition: The type of the left operand of an addition expression shall implement the core::ops::Add trait with the type of the right operand as the trait implementation type parameter.

terms.glossary_definition: A safety invariant is an invariant that when violated may result in undefined behavior.
terms.chapter_definition: It is a safety invariant for the function body to respect the ABI of the function.

terms.glossary_definition: A scalar type is either a bool type, a char type, or a numeric type.
terms.chapter_definition: An arithmetic overflow occurs when an operator expression computes a value of a scalar type that lies outside of the range of valid [value]s for the scalar type or when one or more operand of an operator expression lies outside of the range of valid [value]s for the operation.

terms.glossary_definition: A scope is a region of program text where a name can be referred to.
terms.chapter_definition: A scope is a region of program text where an entity can be referred to. An entity is in scope when it can be referred to.

terms.glossary_definition: A self parameter is a function parameter expressed by keyword ``self``.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_k1vc9vd8at92 Is a method that does not use Self in its function signature except in the type of its self parameter or as the type of a type bound predicate, and

terms.glossary_definition: A separator is a character or a string that separates adjacent [lexical element]s.
terms.chapter_definition: Repetition in a macro matcher is matched based on how many times the pattern appears consecutively optionally separated by a separator in the TokenTree of the macro invocation, as follows:

terms.glossary_definition: A shared reference is a value of a shared reference type.
terms.chapter_definition: A shared reference type implements the core::marker::Copy trait. Copying a shared reference performs a shallow copy.

terms.glossary_definition: A signed integer type is an integer type whose [value]s denote negative whole numbers, zero, and positive whole numbers.
terms.chapter_definition: #. fls_r02OGonXp93A If the types of both [operand]s are [integer type]s, then the shift right expression evaluates to the value of the left operand whose bits are shifted right by the number of positions the right operand evaluates to. If the type of the left operand is any signed integer type and i

terms.glossary_definition: A simple c string literal is any Unicode character except characters 0x0D (carriage return), 0x22 (quotation mark), 0x5C (reverse solidus) and 0x00 (null byte).
terms.chapter_definition: A simple c string literal is a c string literal where the characters are Unicode characters.

terms.glossary_definition: A simple import is a use import that binds a simple path to a local name by using an optional renaming.
terms.chapter_definition: A simple import is a use import that brings all entities it refers to into scope, optionally with a different name than they are declared with by using a renaming.

terms.glossary_definition: A simple path is a path whose [path segment]s consist of either [identifier]s or certain [keyword]s.
terms.chapter_definition: A self public modifier is a visibility modifier that grants a name private visibility. A self public modifier is equivalent to a simple path public modifier where the simple path denotes keyword ``self``.

terms.glossary_definition: A simple path prefix is the leading simple path of a glob import or a nesting import.
terms.chapter_definition: If a simple path appears in a use import and starts with a path segment expressed as either keyword ``crate``, keyword ``$crate``, keyword ``self``, or keyword ``super``, then the path shall be the simple path prefix of a glob import or a nesting import, or the simple path of a simple import.

terms.glossary_definition: The size of a value is the offset in bytes between successive elements in an array type with the same element type, including any padding for alignment.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_kc3gwj9x3jnr Casting an operand of an integer type to a target integer type of the same size has no effect.

terms.glossary_definition: A size operand is an operand that specifies the size of an array or an array type.
terms.chapter_definition: It is a static error if the size operand of an array repetition constructor or an array type depends on [generic parameter]s.

terms.glossary_definition: A sized type is a type with statically known size.
terms.chapter_definition: A sized type is a type that implements the core::marker::Sized trait.

terms.glossary_definition: A statement is a component of a block expression.
terms.chapter_definition: A terminated macro invocation is a macro invocation that may be used as a statement.

terms.glossary_definition: A static is a value that is associated with a specific memory location.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_ib8p7dfwddx2 The static initializer of a static.

terms.glossary_definition: A static initializer is a construct that provides the value of its related static.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_ib8p7dfwddx2 The static initializer of a static.

terms.glossary_definition: A struct is an item that declares a struct type.
terms.chapter_definition: A struct is an abstract data type declared with a StructDeclaration.

terms.glossary_definition: A struct expression is an expression that constructs an enum value, a struct value, or a union value.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_JBfZuFDQg3mU The base initializer of a struct expression.

terms.glossary_definition: A struct pattern is a pattern that matches an enum value, a struct value, or a union value.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_hj6srmzbobid A struct expression corresponds to a struct pattern with all the [subexpression]s lowered to their corresponding [pattern]s.

terms.glossary_definition: A struct value is a value of a struct type.
terms.chapter_definition: A struct expression is an expression that constructs an enum value, a struct value, or a union value.

terms.glossary_definition: A type is structurally equal when its [value]s can be compared for equality by structure.
terms.chapter_definition: When a path pattern refers to an associated constant or a constant, the type of the associated constant or constant shall be structurally equal.

terms.glossary_definition: A subpattern is a pattern nested within another pattern.
terms.chapter_definition: A subpattern of a tuple struct pattern matches a field of the deconstructee when its position and the position of the field in the deconstructee are the same. Such a subpattern is a matched tuple struct subpattern.

terms.glossary_definition: A subtype is a type with additional constraints.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_z00wtlna6grk The source type is a subtype of the target type.

terms.glossary_definition: Subtyping is a property of [type]s, allowing one type to be used where another type is expected.
terms.chapter_definition: subtyping is the relation that determines when one type may be used in place of another.

terms.glossary_definition: A syntactic category is a nonterminal in the Backus-Naur Form grammar definition of the Rust programming language.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_ceb5a8t6cakr A syntactic category is a grammar symbol denoted in PascalCase, for example:

terms.glossary_definition: A temporary is an anonymous variable produced by some intermediate computation.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_gv4M0DE3OMwk A temporary.

terms.glossary_definition: A trait is an item that describes an interface a type can implement.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_qbmp0blpoxx9 If the previous [path segment]'s resolution context is a trait or a type, then perform path expression resolution implementation candidate lookup for the current path segment and that trait or type.

terms.glossary_definition: A tuple is a value of a tuple type.
terms.chapter_definition: A tuple is a value that wraps [argument operand]s in function trait calls.

terms.notes: Term defined inline within another paragraph; definition is contextual
terms.glossary_definition: A tuple enum variant value is a value of a tuple enum variant.
terms.chapter_definition: A call expression is an expression that invokes a function or constructs a tuple enum variant value or a tuple struct value.

terms.notes: Term defined inline within another paragraph; definition is contextual
terms.glossary_definition: A tuple struct value is a value of a tuple struct type.
terms.chapter_definition: A call expression is an expression that invokes a function or constructs a tuple enum variant value or a tuple struct value.

terms.glossary_definition: A type bound predicate is a construct that specifies [lifetime bound]s and [trait bound]s on a type.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_vmLLL82EQasI Its [associated type]s specify a core::marker::Sized [trait bound] for Self in a type bound predicate.

terms.glossary_definition: Type inference is the process of deducing the expected type of an arbitrary value.
terms.chapter_definition: Type inference is the process of automatically determining the type of [expression]s and [pattern]s within a type inference root.

terms.glossary_definition: A type parameter is a generic parameter for a type.
terms.chapter_definition: The type of the left operand of an addition expression shall implement the core::ops::Add trait with the type of the right operand as the trait implementation type parameter.

terms.glossary_definition: A type specification describes the structure of a type.
terms.chapter_definition: The type specification of a function pointer type introduces a generic parameter scope into the scope hierarchy.

terms.glossary_definition: A unified type is a type produced by type unification.
terms.chapter_definition: * fls_ltEygvWDtHXE If the block expression contains at least one break expression and has a tail expression, then the type is the unified type of the [break type]s of all [break expression]s and the type of the tail expression.

terms.glossary_definition: A union type is an abstract data type similar to a C-like union.
terms.chapter_definition: A union type is an abstract data type that is a sum of other [type]s.

terms.glossary_definition: An unnamed lifetime is a lifetime declared with character 0x5F (low line).
terms.chapter_definition: An unnamed lifetime is the ``'_`` lifetime.

terms.glossary_definition: An unsized type is a type with statically unknown size.
terms.chapter_definition: An unsized type is a type that does not implement the core::marker::Sized trait.
